dependencies.description: Fundamental package for array computing in Python.
dependencies.name: py-numpy

dependencies.description: The PyPA recommended tool for installing Python packages.
dependencies.name: py-pip

dependencies.description:
The typing_extensions module contains both backports of these changes as
well as experimental types that will eventually be added to the typing
module, such as Protocol (see PEP 544 for details about protocols and
static duck typing).
dependencies.name: py-typing-extensions

dependencies.description: A Spack managed Python virtual environment
dependencies.name: python-venv

dependent_to.description:
APEBench is a JAX-based tool to evaluate autoregressive neural emulators
for PDEs on periodic domains in 1d, 2d, and 3d. It comes with an
efficient reference simulator based on spectral methods that is used for
procedural data generation (no need to download large datasets with
APEBench). Since this simulator can also be embedded into emulator
training (e.g., for a "solver-in-the-loop" correction setting), this is
the first benchmark suite to support differentiable physics.
dependent_to.name: py-apebench

dependent_to.description: Chai Discovery tools for AI + protein research.
dependent_to.name: py-chai-lab

dependent_to.description:
Equinox is your one-stop [JAX](https://github.com/google/jax) library,
for everything you need that isn't already in core JAX: - neural
networks (or more generally any model), with easy-to-use PyTorch-like
syntax; - filtered APIs for transformations; - useful PyTree
manipulation routines; - advanced features like runtime errors; and best
of all, Equinox isn't a framework: everything you write in Equinox is
compatible with anything else in JAX or the ecosystem.
dependent_to.name: py-equinox

dependent_to.description: Efficient Differentiable n-d PDE solvers built on top of JAX & Equinox.
dependent_to.name: py-exponax

dependent_to.description:
GPyTorch is a Gaussian process library implemented using PyTorch.
GPyTorch is designed for creating scalable, flexible, and modular
Gaussian process models with ease.
dependent_to.name: py-gpytorch

dependent_to.description:
A linear operator implementation, primarily designed for finite-
dimensional positive definite operators (i.e. kernel matrices).
dependent_to.name: py-linear-operator

dependent_to.description:
A collection of neural architectures for emulating Partial Differential
Equations (PDEs) in JAX agnostic to the spatial dimension (1D, 2D, 3D)
and boundary conditions (Dirichlet, Neumann, Periodic). This package is
built on top of Equinox.
dependent_to.name: py-pdequinox

dependent_to.description:
Convenience abstractions using optax to train neural networks to
autoregressively emulate time-dependent problems taking care of
trajectory subsampling and offering a wide range of training
methodologies (regarding unrolling length and including differentiable
physics).
dependent_to.name: py-trainax

description:
Type annotations and runtime checking for shape and dtype of JAX arrays,
and PyTrees.
name: py-jaxtyping

variants.description: Build systems supported by the package
variants.name: build_system
